stories_covered: Anthropic unveils Mythos, withheld from public due to hacking capabilities
Public software stocks tumble to new lows
Meta debuts Muse Spark from Alex Wang's super intelligence labs
Amazon's Trainium chips used for Mythos training
SaaS incumbents facing margin collapse with AI-only 60% solutions
harry_takes: Mythos represents a step-change in capability—machine guns versus rifles in terms of scale and speed finding vulnerabilities, not just the ability to find them
AI is enabling every single breach possible as more apps are built by AI with poor security defaults
Security stocks falling on Mythos news made no sense; companies will need to invest more in defense if attackers have better tools
Dario Amodei's repeated doomsaying has lost credibility; he's become the boy who cried wolf on job displacement and existential risk
SaaS enterprise software faces a vicious cycle: 60% products can't be monetized as standalone offerings and must bundle with base software
harry_boldest_quote: I can't open the Strait of Hormuz myself, I can't do this like enough already, let me use my tokens.
predictions: Jason Lemkin: If agents are only 60% as good as standalone solutions, you're in a slow death spiral
Jason Lemkin: Without meaningful AI capability, large SaaS companies will revert to terminal value-style valuations, not growth plays
Panel consensus: Enterprise is two-thirds of the AI value game going forward, consumer is one-third or less—opposite of the early internet era
disagreements: Rory and Harry debated whether Dario's safety concerns are sincere versus marketing; Rory argued the vision's intensity genuinely drives company culture and innovation, while Harry expressed fatigue with repeated warnings that haven't materialized
harry_role: Driving narrative with strong takes on Mythos, Dario's credibility, and SaaS doom, while moderating between Rory's defense of Dario and Jason's brutally practical assessment of 60% products.
notable: Jason Lemkin's 60% solution thesis becomes central to the show's entire SaaS valuation framework—if agents are only 60% as good, the company is doomed to either free bundling or slow death.
